title: HaloBraid raises $7M from Seven Seven Six to end the six-hour hair salon appointment
slides: Your salon appointment just doubled without warning. Why?
HaloBraid automates the braiding step that takes hours.
It is a tool, not a robot—and still needs human hands.
Stylists now book two clients in the same time slot.
Call your salon tomorrow: ask if they use HaloBraid.
thread_text:
1. Your bill just doubled

A HaloBraid subscription adds $30 per client, automatically billed to your card.

2. It’s not a robot

A human still feeds the braiding tool strand by strand. No one leaves the room.

3. Stylists double-book now

Two clients in one slot means two payments. The machine runs while the stylist combs the second head.

4. The twist: it’s optional

Salons that adopt it raise prices. Those that don’t lose clients asking for the cheaper two‑hour braid.

5. Call your salon tomorrow

Ask if they use HaloBraid. If they don’t, ask why they left money on the table.
video_script: It’s a tool, not a robot.
Stylists book two clients at once.
Call your salon and ask about it.

script: It’s a tool, not a robot.
Stylists book two clients at once.
Call your salon and ask about it.

instagram.caption:
Your salon appointment just doubled without warning.

HaloBraid automates the braiding step that takes hours. It’s a tool, not a robot—human hands still feed each strand. Stylists now book two clients in the same time slot for double the pay 💸.

This means salons can raise prices or keep the same fee for half the time, passing the cost to you with a $30 subscription fee.

Call your salon tomorrow: ask if they use HaloBraid—or if they’re leaving money and your time on the table ⏳👀
